+title: 'entity-cache follow-up: close list_task <5ms gap by caching derived compute-field values on CachedEntity (Option B)'
+---
+#entity-cache
+
+Parent context: `01KP7K4NMJRET0J4SQQ8950M6H` (entity-cache 3/4) met its primary <20ms `MoveTask` target by caching compute *inputs* (`_changelog`, `_file_created`) but did not meet its secondary <5ms `list_task` sanity-check target. Actual measurement after Option A landed:
+
+- `list_task` (single `ectx.list("task")` on 2000 tasks): median **18.95ms** (target <5ms).
+
+Option A eliminated the per-task I/O â€” the compute-input cache short-circuits `read_changelog` and `stat` on cache hits â€” but the compute-engine itself still takes ~18ms for 2000 tasks with 4 system-date fields each. That is the remaining dominant cost.
+
+## What
+
+The compute engine (`ComputeEngine::derive_all` in `swissarmyhammer-fields`) runs on every `list()` call for every entity. For the `task` type it derives 4 system-date fields (`created`, `updated`, `started`, `completed`) by parsing the cached `_changelog` array. The inputs are identical across repeat calls (until the entity mutates), so the *outputs* are also identical â€” they are prime caching targets.
+
+This card is Option B from the parent card's planning section: cache the computed-field outputs alongside the entity in `CachedEntity` and invalidate them in the same mutation paths already wired for Option A.
+
+## Approach
+
+Add a `derived: Option<IndexMap<FieldName, serde_json::Value>>` slot to `CachedEntity` (or a sibling map keyed by `(entity_type, id)` â€” whichever avoids lock contention with the existing primary `cache` RwLock). Populate it lazily on first `list()`/`read()` when a compute pass produces values. Invalidate it from every mutation path that already invalidates the compute-input cache:
+
+- `write` / `refresh_from_disk` / `unarchive` â€” clear the derived slot (next compute pass repopulates)
+- `delete` / `evict` / `archive` â€” purge the derived slot (entity is gone)
+
+The epoch-based race protection already in place for `compute_inputs` is the template â€” either reuse the same epoch or add a sibling `derived_epoch` depending on how independent the two caches need to be.
+
+## Files
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” add the derived-output map and invalidation hooks
+- `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs` â€” `apply_compute_batch` must check the derived cache before running the compute engine, and write results back into the cache after running it
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/benches/move_task_bench.rs` â€” existing `list_task` diagnostic bench is the acceptance driver
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `list_task` diagnostic bench median <5ms on a 2000-task seeded board (target inherited from parent card).
+- [ ] `move_task_2000/with_before` median stays <20ms (no regression on the primary target Option A already hit).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` stays green.
+- [ ] Cache invalidation is proven end-to-end: a `write` on task A must invalidate the cached derived values for A and cause the next `list`/`read` to observe the re-derived values. Regression test alongside the existing `test_changelog_cache_*` suite.
+- [ ] `EntityChanged` event shape remains stable (same contract as Option A preserved).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” `test_derived_cache_memoizes_across_calls`: list twice, assert the second pass does not invoke the compute engine (counter or `inject_compute_dependencies` spy).
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” `test_derived_cache_invalidates_on_write`: list, write, list, assert the post-write derived values reflect the new entity state.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” mirror tests for `_on_delete` / `_on_evict` / `_on_archive` / `_on_refresh_from_disk` â€” same matrix as the Option A tests.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/benches/move_task_bench.rs` â€” `list_task` bench closes the target.
+
+## Baseline (as of parent card completion)
+
+- `list_task` (2000 tasks): 18.95ms median â€” **this card closes the gap to <5ms**
+- `move_task_2000/with_before`: 19.53ms median â€” must stay under 20ms
+
+## Depends on
+
+- `01KP7K4NMJRET0J4SQQ8950M6H` â€” Option A (compute-input cache) must land first; this card extends the invalidation framework established there.

#entity-cache

Parent context: `01KP7K4NMJRET0J4SQQ8950M6H` (entity-cache 3/4) met its primary <20ms `MoveTask` target by caching compute *inputs* (`_changelog`, `_file_created`) but did not meet its secondary <5ms `list_task` sanity-check target. Actual measurement after Option A landed:

- `list_task` (single `ectx.list("task")` on 2000 tasks): median **18.95ms** (target <5ms).

Option A eliminated the per-task I/O â€” the compute-input cache short-circuits `read_changelog` and `stat` on cache hits â€” but the compute-engine itself still takes ~18ms for 2000 tasks with 4 system-date fields each. That is the remaining dominant cost.

## What

The compute engine (`ComputeEngine::derive_all` in `swissarmyhammer-fields`) runs on every `list()` call for every entity. For the `task` type it derives 4 system-date fields (`created`, `updated`, `started`, `completed`) by parsing the cached `_changelog` array. The inputs are identical across repeat calls (until the entity mutates), so the *outputs* are also identical â€” they are prime caching targets.

This card is Option B from the parent card's planning section: cache the computed-field outputs alongside the entity in `CachedEntity` and invalidate them in the same mutation paths already wired for Option A.

## Approach

Add a `derived: Option<IndexMap<FieldName, serde_json::Value>>` slot to `CachedEntity` (or a sibling map keyed by `(entity_type, id)` â€” whichever avoids lock contention with the existing primary `cache` RwLock). Populate it lazily on first `list()`/`read()` when a compute pass produces values. Invalidate it from every mutation path that already invalidates the compute-input cache:

- `write` / `refresh_from_disk` / `unarchive` â€” clear the derived slot (next compute pass repopulates)
- `delete` / `evict` / `archive` â€” purge the derived slot (entity is gone)

The epoch-based race protection already in place for `compute_inputs` is the template â€” either reuse the same epoch or add a sibling `derived_epoch` depending on how independent the two caches need to be.

## Files

- `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” add the derived-output map and invalidation hooks
- `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs` â€” `apply_compute_batch` must check the derived cache before running the compute engine, and write results back into the cache after running it
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/benches/move_task_bench.rs` â€” existing `list_task` diagnostic bench is the acceptance driver

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `list_task` diagnostic bench median <5ms on a 2000-task seeded board (target inherited from parent card).
- [ ] `move_task_2000/with_before` median stays <20ms (no regression on the primary target Option A already hit).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-entity -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` stays green.
- [ ] Cache invalidation is proven end-to-end: a `write` on task A must invalidate the cached derived values for A and cause the next `list`/`read` to observe the re-derived values. Regression test alongside the existing `test_changelog_cache_*` suite.
- [ ] `EntityChanged` event shape remains stable (same contract as Option A preserved).

## Tests

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” `test_derived_cache_memoizes_across_calls`: list twice, assert the second pass does not invoke the compute engine (counter or `inject_compute_dependencies` spy).
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” `test_derived_cache_invalidates_on_write`: list, write, list, assert the post-write derived values reflect the new entity state.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs` â€” mirror tests for `_on_delete` / `_on_evict` / `_on_archive` / `_on_refresh_from_disk` â€” same matrix as the Option A tests.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/benches/move_task_bench.rs` â€” `list_task` bench closes the target.

## Baseline (as of parent card completion)

- `list_task` (2000 tasks): 18.95ms median â€” **this card closes the gap to <5ms**
- `move_task_2000/with_before`: 19.53ms median â€” must stay under 20ms

## Depends on

- `01KP7K4NMJRET0J4SQQ8950M6H` â€” Option A (compute-input cache) must land first; this card extends the invalidation framework established there.

+## Implementation Summary

-- `list_task` (2000 tasks): 18.95ms median â€” **this card closes the gap to <5ms**
-- `move_task_2000/with_before`: 19.53ms median â€” must stay under 20ms
+- **Primary cache change** (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs`): Added a third per-entity map, `derived: RwLock<HashMap<(String, String), CachedDerived>>`, that memoizes every computed-field output â€” both simple derivations (`created`, `updated`, `started`, `completed`, `status_date`, `progress`, `virtual_tags`, `filter_tags` on `task`) and aggregates (`tags`). Renamed the existing `compute_inputs_epoch` to `cache_epoch` since it now guards both secondary caches. Renamed `invalidate_compute_inputs` â†’ `invalidate_entity_caches` and `purge_compute_inputs` â†’ `purge_entity_caches` since they now clear both the inputs and the derived outputs.
+- **Cross-entity invalidation** (new): Aggregate derivations can query sibling entity types, so entity-local invalidation alone is unsafe. Added `invalidate_cross_type_derived(trigger_type)` which consults `FieldsContext::entity_types_depending_on(trigger_type)` and clears the derived-output slots of every entity type whose aggregate fields declare `depends_on: [trigger_type]`. Called from both `invalidate_entity_caches` and `purge_entity_caches`, so every mutation path (write/delete/evict/archive/unarchive/refresh_from_disk) propagates correctly.
+- **Compute path change** (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs`): `derive_compute_fields` now fetches cached outputs at the top of each per-entity call. On a warm hit it copies cached values into `entity.fields` and skips both the pseudo-field injection (`_changelog` / `_file_created`) and the compute-engine run entirely. On a cold miss it runs the derivations in declaration order (preserving the `derive_all` ordering contract) and memoizes the results under the epoch guard.
+- **Field definition bug fix** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/tags.yaml`): added `depends_on: [tag]` to the `tags` computed field. Without this, `FieldsContext::entity_types_depending_on("tag")` would return nothing and writing a tag would never invalidate the cached `task.tags` values. This declaration is required by the cross-type invalidation mechanism â€” fixing it is what lets the `parse-body-tags` aggregate be cached correctly.
+
+## Baseline vs result
+
+| Bench | Baseline (Option A) | Target | Result |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `list_task` (2000 tasks) | 18.95ms | <5ms | **3.37ms** |
+| `move_task_2000/with_before` | 19.53ms | <20ms | **3.61ms** |
